13486.R1_justification: Recounts forgetting to sell a concert ticket and the resulting misunderstanding with a friend as external facts; 'i apologized so from my side its resolved' is guarded closure with no participant-originated inner state.
13486.R2_justification: Multi-sentence factual account with context but entirely external, no inner state.
13486.disclosure_start: We had tickets for a concert but i didnt really listened to the band

13750.R1_justification: Group-project conflict where participant originates a fear ('i am scared that others would think i am a drama queen') and uncertainty ('i dont know what to do rn'), revealing inner state beyond the facts.
13750.R2_justification: Genuinely engages with inner state (fear of being seen as a drama queen, indecision), not just naming an emotion.
13750.disclosure_start: i am scared that others would think i am a drama queen

13849.R1_justification: Detailed romantic-relationship disagreement about trip planning; participant originates annoyance and relational friction ('which is a little annoying', repeated unmet promises), some inner state but stays about the incident.
13849.R2_justification: Inner state (annoyance, worry about budget/relationship) engaged beyond mere naming, person visible behind the facts.
13849.disclosure_start: we had a disagreement on our summer plans

14260.R1_justification: Complaint about an econometrics tutor who couldn't teach; 'boring and exhausting' refers to the course, not personal inner state. Entirely external.
14260.R2_justification: Reasonably elaborated factual account of the course/tutor, no inner state.
14260.disclosure_start: our tutor was not the best so we didnt learn much there

14614.R1_justification: Friend/gas-money conflict; participant originates emotion and self-reflection ('i was feeling bad to ask her', 'i end up a bit shouting', 'i feel so bad to ask money', 'i hate depend on people').
14614.R2_justification: Engages inner state and reflects on own reaction (guilt, shouting, valuing independence), not just named.
14614.disclosure_start: she didnt want to pay the gas but i was feeling bad to ask her

14764.R1_justification: Roommate-choice situation that 'all worked out good at the end'; purely external resolution with no inner state originated by the participant.
14764.R2_justification: Situation with basic detail, entirely external, no inner state.
14764.disclosure_start: I had to decide on which of my friends I was going to live with

14665.R1_justification: Roommate-tidiness conflict where participant originates inner state: 'i feel like that i am always the one cleaning', 'i got annoyed and just stayed at a friends house', apologies but 'still nothing really changed'.
14665.R2_justification: Engages inner state (feeling unfairly burdened, annoyance, acting on it), not merely named.
14665.disclosure_start: i feel like that i am always the one cleaning up

14767.R1_justification: Unreliable tutorial-group member; participant originates the uncertainty 'I dont really know how to approach this situation and how to tell him', which the rubric counts as an inner-state signal tipping to 2.
14767.R2_justification: Uncertainty about how to act is engaged briefly rather than just an external complaint.
14767.disclosure_start: I dont really know how to approach this situation

14413.R1_justification: Feeling like an outsider with flatmates; participant originates a genuine feeling of loneliness ('sometimes it make me feel alone', 'i m not a jealuse person but'), brief but self-revealing inner state.
14413.R2_justification: Loneliness/exclusion engaged as inner state, though still fairly guarded.
14413.disclosure_start: sometimes i fell like we are a little separate

14236.R1_justification: Romantic interest who grew distant; participant originates confusion and rumination ('i didnt understand what i did wrong', still thinking about her, deciding to keep distance) before drifting into an EV/car tangent.
14236.R2_justification: Inner state (confusion, lingering preoccupation) engaged before the topic shifts, person visible beyond facts.
14236.disclosure_start: it got more distand and i didnt understand what i did wrong

14110.R1_justification: Drunken night-out fallout with a friend; strong participant-originated self-reflection ('i wasnt really acting as myself', 'i definitely overreacted', 'i still said some things i probably shouldnt have') with ongoing relational consequence, but stays composed/about the incident.
14110.R2_justification: Substantive engagement with own behaviour and reaction, reflective rather than raw or self-exposing.
14110.disclosure_start: i personally wasnt really acting as myself

14815.R1_justification: Roommate kitchen-cleanliness disagreement; participant originates inner state ('I feel disrespected', 'It feels very bad and I dont know what to do about it') beyond the external facts.
14815.R2_justification: Engages inner state (feeling disrespected, hurt by hypocrisy, helplessness), not just named.
14815.disclosure_start: Im a really clean person and I feel disrespected

14848.R1_justification: Ex-friend confronting the participant at a festival after spreading rumours; participant originates 'i was more disappointed because she used to tell me that I'm her best friend' and 'treated me more as a servant and personal diary than as a friend' — genuine relational hurt.
14848.R2_justification: Disappointment and felt betrayal engaged with relational meaning, though it stays focused on the incident rather than raw self-exposure.
14848.disclosure_start: she used to tell me that I'm her best friend

16495.R1_justification: Trivial argument with a friend over Barcelona vs Real Madrid; only external description ('heated', 'he was really angry') and a request for advice, no participant inner state.
16495.R2_justification: Situation named with minimal external detail across short turns, no inner state.
16495.disclosure_start: I had a argument with a friend of mine today

15046.R1_justification: Largely a positive recount of attending an event without her friend and meeting someone new; tips to 2 only via the self-originated reflection 'I still need to get used to it. sometimes i'm very comfortable and other times not at all'.
15046.R2_justification: Emotional/self-aware tone (social discomfort) present but only lightly engaged, mostly external positive narrative.
15046.disclosure_start: I still need to get used to it. sometimes i'm very confortable and other times not at all

14041.R1_justification: Telling a friend she couldn't attend her birthday; participant originates fear and avoidance ('i was afraid of her reaction', 'i dragged it a long time because i was afraid', 'i didnt want to dissapoint her').
14041.R2_justification: Inner state (anxiety, avoidance, relief) engaged and reflected on, not merely named.
14041.disclosure_start: i didnt want to dissapoint her
